pois.name: Schloss Sanssouci
pois.description: The summer palace of Frederick the Great, renowned for its Rococo architecture and terraced vineyards, completed in 1747.

pois.name: Kloster Chorin
pois.description: A former Cistercian abbey and a prime example of North German Brick Gothic architecture, founded in 1258.

pois.name: Kloster Neuzelle
pois.description: One of the few preserved Cistercian monasteries in Central Europe with a rich Baroque interior known as the 'Baroque Wonder of Brandenburg'.

pois.name: Schloss Cecilienhof
pois.description: The site of the Potsdam Conference in 1945, this English country house style palace was the last built by the Hohenzollerns.

pois.name: Schloss Rheinsberg
pois.description: A picturesque palace on the shores of Lake Grienerick, which served as a model for Sanssouci and inspired literary works by Fontane.

pois.name: Schloss Branitz
pois.description: The residence of Prince Hermann von Pückler-Muskau, featuring a unique English landscape park with earth pyramids.

pois.name: Kloster Zinna
pois.description: A former Cistercian monastery featuring a well-preserved late Romanesque church and a museum on the history of the abbey.

pois.name: Kloster Lehnin
pois.description: The oldest Cistercian monastery in the Margraviate of Brandenburg, displaying the transition from Romanesque to Gothic architecture.

pois.name: Stift Heiligengrabe
pois.description: A remarkably well-preserved Cistercian convent complex with a rare octagonal Holy Grave chapel.

pois.name: Schloss Boitzenburg
pois.description: One of the largest aristocratic estates in Brandenburg, featuring a Neo-Renaissance facade and a scenic landscape park.

pois.name: Schloss Oranienburg
pois.description: The oldest Baroque palace in Brandenburg, named after Louise Henriette of Orange-Nassau, featuring a porcelain museum.

pois.name: Schloss Caputh
pois.description: A summer residence of the Great Elector, known for its unique Tile Hall decorated with about 7,500 Dutch tiles.

pois.name: Schloss Paretz
pois.description: The rural retreat of Queen Luise of Prussia, designed in a simple Neoclassical style to resemble a noble farmhouse.

pois.name: Burg Rabenstein
pois.description: A high medieval hilltop castle in the Fläming region, known for its preserved keep and historic falconry.

pois.name: Burg Eisenhardt
pois.description: A significant fortification in Bad Belzig, serving as a border fortress between the Margraviate of Brandenburg and Saxony.

pois.name: Brandenburger Dom St. Peter und Paul
pois.description: The mother church of the state of Brandenburg, located on an island and featuring rare medieval wall paintings and a historic library.

pois.name: Wunderblutkirche Bad Wilsnack
pois.description: A major European pilgrimage destination in the Middle Ages, known for its grand proportions and the legend of the bleeding hosts.

pois.name: St. Marienkirche Prenzlau
pois.description: A monumental Brick Gothic hall church in the Uckermark, famous for its magnificent eastern gable.

pois.name: Nikolaikirche Potsdam
pois.description: A prominent Neoclassical church designed by Karl Friedrich Schinkel, dominating the skyline of Potsdam with its large dome.

pois.name: Stadtmauer Wittstock
pois.description: The only fully preserved brick city wall in Germany, surrounding the historic center for approximately 2.5 kilometers.

pois.name: Altstadt Jüterbog
pois.description: A historic town center featuring three massive medieval city gates and numerous buildings associated with the Reformation.

pois.name: Altstadt Luckau
pois.description: One of the best-preserved historic town centers in Brandenburg, often called the 'Pearl of Lower Lusatia' for its Baroque townhouses.

pois.name: Altstadt Brandenburg an der Havel
pois.description: Comprising three historic cores on the Havel river, it features the iconic Old Town Hall and several medieval churches.

pois.name: Altstadt Templin
pois.description: Known as the 'Pearl of the Uckermark', the town is enclosed by a massive 1,735-meter long medieval stone wall with gates and towers.

pois.name: Altstadt Angermünde
pois.description: A historic town with a regular medieval grid, timber-framed houses, and a market square featuring a traditional town hall.

pois.name: Altstadt Perleberg
pois.description: The historic center of the Prignitz region, located on an island in the Stepenitz river, featuring a Roland statue and Gothic town hall.

pois.name: Altstadt Beeskow
pois.description: A well-preserved town on the Spree river with a medieval castle, city wall, and the large St. Mary's Church.

pois.name: Schloss Neuhardenberg
pois.description: A Neoclassical palace complex redesigned by Schinkel, known for its association with the Prussian reformer Karl August von Hardenberg.

pois.name: Schloss Königs Wusterhausen
pois.description: The favorite residence of the 'Soldier King' Frederick William I, known for his 'Tobacco Cabinet' meetings.

pois.name: Schloss Meseberg
pois.description: A Baroque palace that serves as the official guest house of the German Federal Government.

pois.name: Schloss Wolfshagen
pois.description: A late Baroque manor house in the Prignitz, featuring rare painted wall coverings and a museum of noble culture.

pois.name: Schloss Wiesenburg
pois.description: A mix of Renaissance and Neo-Renaissance architecture featuring a prominent tower and one of the finest landscape parks in the region.

pois.name: Freilichtmuseum Lehde
pois.description: An open-air museum in the Spreewald showing traditional Sorbian/Wendish farmsteads accessible by waterways.

pois.name: Ribbeck
pois.description: The village famous for Theodor Fontane's poem about the pear tree, featuring the historic manor house of the von Ribbeck family.

pois.name: Baumkronenpfad Beelitz-Heilstätten
pois.description: A treetop walk winding over the ruins of a massive former tuberculosis sanatorium overgrown by nature.

pois.name: Glienicker Brücke
pois.description: Known as the 'Bridge of Spies', this crossing between Potsdam and Berlin was the site of Cold War prisoner exchanges.

pois.name: Großer Stechlinsee
pois.description: An oligotrophic lake famous for its water clarity and being the setting of Fontane's final novel, 'Der Stechlin'.

pois.name: Scharmützelsee
pois.description: Often called the 'Märkisches Meer', this large lake is a central hub for water sports and health tourism in Brandenburg.

pois.name: Werbellinsee
pois.description: A deep glacial lake within the Schorfheide-Chorin Biosphere Reserve, historically a preferred hunting ground for German leaders.

pois.name: Bismarckturm Rathenow
pois.description: An observation tower built on the Weinberg hill providing panoramic views of the Havelland region.

pois.name: Besucherbergwerk F60
pois.description: Known as the 'Lying Eiffel Tower of Lusatia', it is the world's largest movable technical industrial machine.

pois.name: Oderbruch Museum Altranft
pois.description: A museum dedicated to the unique cultural landscape of the Oderbruch, documenting the land reclamation under Frederick the Great.

pois.name: Burg Friedland
pois.description: One of the best-preserved medieval lowland castles in the region, serving as a cultural center for the town of Friedland.

pois.name: Burg Storkow
pois.description: A 12th-century lowland castle that has been restored to house a visitor center for the Dahme-Heideseen Nature Park.

pois.name: Kloster Alexanderdorf
pois.description: The first Benedictine monastery for nuns in Brandenburg since the Reformation, established in a former manor house.

pois.name: Schloss Demerthin
pois.description: An impressive Renaissance palace in the Prignitz, notable for its largely original exterior and stepped gables.

pois.name: Schloss Steinhöfel
pois.description: A Neoclassical palace ensemble surrounded by a landscape park that was influential for Prussian garden design.

pois.name: Schloss Dannenwalde
pois.description: A Baroque manor house known for the nearby octagonal Patronatskirche, a rare architectural form in the region.

pois.name: Plattenburg
pois.description: The oldest water castle in Northern Germany, once the summer residence of the Bishops of Havelberg.

pois.name: Burg Lenzen
pois.description: A historic castle on the Elbe river, now hosting a center for floodplain ecology and a museum on regional history.

pois.name: Slawenburg Raddusch
pois.description: A modern reconstruction of a 9th-century Slavic ring fort, housing an archaeological museum about the Lusatian region.

pois.name: Freilichtmuseum Höllberghof
pois.description: A reconstructed historic farmstead showing the rural lifestyle and building techniques of the Fläming region around 1800.

pois.name: Telegrafenberg Potsdam
pois.description: A science park home to the Einstein Tower and the Great Refractor, significant for the history of astrophysics.

pois.name: Schloss Wiepersdorf
pois.description: The former home of the Romantic poets Achim and Bettina von Arnim, now serving as an artist residence and museum.

pois.name: St. Marien Bernau
pois.description: A Late Gothic hall church with a significant winged altar from the workshop of Lucas Cranach the Elder.

pois.name: Stadtpfarrkirche Müncheberg
pois.description: A Schinkel-designed church within a historic town wall, featuring a modern integrated library and cultural space.

pois.name: Schloss Großkmehlen
pois.description: A Renaissance water castle in the extreme south of the state, known for its ornate gables and historic interiors.

pois.name: Festungsturm Peitz
pois.description: The massive red-brick tower is the last remains of a 16th-century fortress that once protected the border to Saxony.

pois.name: Kloster Marienstern Mühlberg
pois.description: A former Cistercian nunnery located near the site of the historic Battle of Mühlberg in 1547.

pois.name: Schloss Liebenberg
pois.description: The former estate of the Eulenburg family, infamous for the 'Liebenberg Circle' and often visited by Kaiser Wilhelm II.
